model_rationale: Chlamydia trachomatis infection is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection unrelated to Zika virus, which is a mosquito-borne flavivirus. While both are infectious diseases, they have entirely different etiologies, epidemiology, transmission routes, and clinical manifestations. The high embedding similarity (0.8656) appears to be a false positive driven by shared terminology around infectious disease classification rather than genuine clinical or genetic overlap.
